active_question: Which websites are recommended for U.S.-based rope conventions?
edited_answer: Tethered Together (https://tetheredtogether.net/) combines rope workshops with circus, kink, relationship, movement, attendee-led, art, and entertainment programming. ROPECRAFT (https://ropecraft.net/) emphasizes multi-style, beginner-through-advanced role education plus peer support, special-interest, practice, and self-tying spaces; both describe participant-driven Ropenspace learning.
edited_question: Which United States rope conventions are owner-recommended, and how do their inspected programming emphases differ?
paraphrase_rationale: The original answer preserved both convention links but did not distinguish them. The replacement keeps both exact owner-recommended URLs and summarizes inspected programming and support differences.
reason: The original answer preserved both convention links but did not distinguish them. The replacement keeps both exact owner-recommended URLs and summarizes inspected programming and support differences.
support_evidence:
Tethered Together is https://tetheredtogether.net/. Its inspected site describes rope-focused workshops, broader circus, kink, relationship, dance, and yoga programming, attendee-led content, art, entertainment, and a participant-driven Ropenspace.
Tethered Together is https://tetheredtogether.net/. Its inspected site describes rope-focused workshops, broader circus, kink, relationship, dance, and yoga programming, attendee-led content, art, entertainment, and a participant-driven Ropenspace.
ROPECRAFT is https://ropecraft.net/. Its inspected site describes beginner-through-advanced education for tops, bottoms, and switches across several rope styles, plus Ropenspace, peer support, special-interest groups, practice and self-tying spaces, and an inclusive consent-oriented event model.
